carbon_policy_signal.item: EU greenhouse gas emissions rose in 2025; stagnant decarbonization progress triggers tighter phase-III/IV ETS caps and accelerated coal phase-out pressure.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Missed targets will force regulator to tighten EUA issuance in future phases and/or accelerate coal retirement, supporting thermal generation margins and raising EUA floor despite current neutral percentile (39th).

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (180 days old, as-of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark stale (175 days old, as-of 2025-12-31) — cannot assess current merit-order fuel-switch or curve steepness; rely on live TTF (51st pctile), DE Power (89th pctile), GB Power (99th pctile), and Clean Spark (99th pctile) as proxy.
risk_flags: Storage at 46.97% full, 14.5 pp below seasonal norm (data: 2026-06-22) — refill pace critical for H2 stability; heat stress may delay summer destocking.
Clean Spark surged +39.39 EUR/MWh daily (+125.59 monthly); extended 3.5σ above 50d trend — tail-risk if cooling-water constraints bite or generation outages spike.
Coal data stale (180 days old, as-of 2025-12-26); cannot assess current merit-order rank — rely on TTF (51st percentile, neutral) and DE Power (89th percentile) as live proxy.
EU emissions rose in 2025 per news — may trigger tighter ETS caps by phase IV, supporting EUA floor; current EUA (39th percentile, 33.29 EUR/tCO2) underprices policy tail.

base.summary: Heat stress sustains peak demand through week; storage steady-state; TTF neutral; Clean Spark elevated but stable.

downside.summary: Heat wave abates or demand softens; LNG cargoes divert to EU; storage injection accelerates; mechanical relief from coal/gas.

upside.summary: Cooling-water constraints trigger nuclear/hydro outages; LNG Asia bidding tightens cargoes further; storage refill slows.

themes: Extreme heat driving peak power demand and intraday margin compression
Storage deficit (22nd percentile) constraining gas flexibility; LNG competition rising
Coal at 7th-percentile (stale data, 180 days old) — fuel-switch regime tilted to gas/renewables
GB Power at 99th-percentile (162.81 EUR/MWh) — island premium widening on tight supply
top_drivers: Extreme heat wave across Europe (intraday horizon) — peak cooling load surge compressing DA margins and lifting real-time premiums; Clean Spark at 99th-pctile already pricing near-capacity stress.

Storage deficit (46.97%, 14.5 pp below seasonal) — limits gas swing capacity; combined with rising LNG competition tightening global import windows, TTF upside tail-risk if summer demand surprises or geopolitical shocks cut flows.

GB Power at 99th-percentile (162.81 EUR/MWh) — island supply/demand imbalance, interconnector constraints, and UK political uncertainty post-Starmer widening GB-DE premium; impacts EU wholesale price floor via arbitrage.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 99th-percentile (118.24 EUR/MWh) amid extreme heat and low storage (47% full, 14.5 pp below seasonal); gas weakly bid despite LNG competition tightening.

watchlist.why: Peak cooling demand shock raises real-time and DA power spreads; hydro/nuclear outage risk if water temps exceed cooling thresholds; Clean Spark (99th pctile) vulnerable to supply disruptions.
Weakened standards extend gas competitiveness vs. coal/renewables, keeping power generation margins compressed and EUA-gas arb tight; horizon months, but market pricing in regulatory relief already.

watchlist.metric: LNG competition (Asia bidding up global cargoes)
watchlist.why: Tightens global liquefaction capacity; direct upward pressure on TTF via LNG arb (currently flat at 51st percentile); reduced NW Europe volumes feed through to power fuel costs and Clean Spark upside.
ETS linkage and emissions-trading deal timing now uncertain; impacts UKA-EUA spread and bilateral carbon-credit flows; UK political instability adds tail-risk to EU energy regulatory alignment.
At 22nd percentile; summer injections lagging seasonal; tight margins for H2 winter reserve build if demand persists or geopolitical supply shocks emerge.

watchlist_dated.name: European Council: China trade agenda
watchlist_dated.why: Energy-tech supply-chain outcome may constrain renewables rollout; indirect upside to power prices if decarbonization delays.
